summary: No wireframe exists for /admin/users or /admin/users/[id] (route-without-wireframe, as flagged in BRIEF.json). Visual conformance (§3.2.1) could only be assessed against product/security sense, not a design spec.
reproduction: 1. Search design/screens/ for any admin-users wireframe file.
2. Confirm BRIEF.json screen.wireframe_files is an empty array and coverage_gap = 'route-without-wireframe'.
expected: A wireframe file to compare the rendered /admin/users and /admin/users/[id] screens against.
observed: No wireframe file exists anywhere in the repo for these routes; brief confirms this is a known, pre-flagged coverage gap.

summary: CLM-101 claims the admin panel provides a data export/delete capability for user data. Live API probing found no backing endpoint for this on the admin-users surface: GET /admin/users/{id}/export -> 404 Not Found; DELETE /admin/users/{id} -> 405 Method Not Allowed (only GET is registered on that path). Full source review of apps/api/app/routers/admin.py (all 10 admin operations) and apps/api/app/main.py (every mounted router) shows no export/GDPR/delete-user route anywhere in the API. Could not be fully closed because rendering the real admin-users UI is HUMAN-GATED (no operator-admin credential), so a dead/non-functional UI button cannot be ruled out independently of the missing backend -- filed as UNSURE, not a flat refutation.
reproduction: 1. curl -X GET https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/api/admin/users/test-id/export -> 404 Not Found (run twice, identical both times).
2. curl -X DELETE https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/api/admin/users/test-id -> 405 Method Not Allowed (run twice, identical both times).

3. Read apps/api/app/routers/admin.py in full: 10 operations total (health, list users, user detail, spend-cap, suspend, unsuspend, spend overview, get settings, update settings, audit-log) -- none named export/delete-user.
4. Read apps/api/app/main.py include_router calls: no export/gdpr router mounted anywhere in the API.
expected: If the admin panel provides a data export/delete capability, a backing endpoint should exist and respond meaningfully (200/202) rather than 404/405.
observed: No such endpoint exists anywhere in the deployed API surface (live-probed twice, and confirmed absent by full source review).
